title: Applications of the Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic
book: Applications of the Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic

node_types.description: A human individual involved in clinical mental health, including patients, therapists, contributors, and editors.

properties.name: full_name
properties.description: The person's full name

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role in the clinical context (e.g., patient, therapist, contributor, editor)

properties.name: credentials
properties.description: Professional degrees or certifications

properties.name: affiliation
properties.description: Institutional or organizational affiliation

properties.name: cultural_identity
properties.description: Cultural, ethnic, or religious identity relevant to treatment

properties.name: demographics
properties.description: Demographic information such as age, gender, etc.

supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A mental health disorder characterized by emotional dysregulation, including anxiety, mood, and related disorders.

properties.name: disorder_name
properties.description: Name of the disorder (e.g., Major Depressive Disorder, PTSD)

properties.name: dsm_code
properties.description: DSM-5 or ICD code, if applicable

properties.name: description
properties.description: Brief description of the disorder

properties.name: core_features
properties.description: Key symptoms or features

properties.name: comorbidities
properties.description: Common comorbid disorders

properties.name: cultural_variants
properties.description: Cultural or regional variants of the disorder

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders
16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A core or optional module of the Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment, representing a treatment component.

properties.name: module_name
properties.description: Name of the module (e.g., Mindful Emotion Awareness)

properties.name: module_type
properties.description: Core or optional module

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the module's focus and techniques

properties.name: targeted_processes
properties.description: Transdiagnostic processes or symptoms targeted

properties.name: recommended_order
properties.description: Suggested order in treatment sequence

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders
15 Group Treatment Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A standardized instrument or scale used to assess symptoms, severity, or treatment progress.

properties.name: tool_name
properties.description: Name of the assessment tool (e.g., OASIS, ODSIS, Y-BOCS-II)

properties.name: measured_constructs
properties.description: Symptoms or domains measured

properties.name: format
properties.description: Format (e.g., self-report, clinician-administered)

properties.name: cultural_adaptations
properties.description: Available cultural or language adaptations

supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A structured plan outlining the course of treatment for a patient, including selected modules, goals, and adaptations.

properties.name: plan_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the treatment plan

properties.name: goals
properties.description: Treatment goals and objectives

properties.name: selected_modules
properties.description: List of Unified Protocol modules included

properties.name: cultural_considerations
properties.description: Cultural or religious adaptations (e.g., Islamic alignment)

properties.name: format
properties.description: Delivery format (e.g., individual, group, technology-assisted)

properties.name: duration
properties.description: Planned duration or number of sessions

supported_by_chapters: 15 Group Treatment Applications of the Unified Protocol
16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A specific psychological, emotional, or behavioral symptom relevant to emotional disorders.

properties.name: symptom_name
properties.description: Name of the symptom (e.g., avoidance, rumination, hyperarousal)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the symptom

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity rating (if available)

properties.name: duration
properties.description: Duration or frequency

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders

node_types.description: A set of cultural, religious, or social factors relevant to case formulation and treatment adaptation.

properties.name: context_name
properties.description: Name or description of the cultural context (e.g., Japanese, Islamic, Colombian)

properties.name: relevant_factors
properties.description: Beliefs, values, or practices impacting mental health or treatment

properties.name: assessment_tools
properties.description: Tools used to assess cultural context (e.g., CFI, MEIM)

properties.name: adaptation_type
properties.description: Surface or deep structure adaptation

supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

node_types.description: A psychological process or mechanism involved in the regulation of emotions, such as avoidance, cognitive reappraisal, or mindfulness.

properties.name: process_name
properties.description: Name of the process (e.g., experiential avoidance, cognitive flexibility)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the process

properties.name: maladaptive_or_adaptive
properties.description: Whether the process is considered maladaptive, adaptive, or context-dependent

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders

node_types.description: A structured clinical conceptualization of a patient's presenting problems, vulnerabilities, and maintaining factors.

properties.name: formulation_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the case formulation

properties.name: presenting_problems
properties.description: List of presenting problems or symptoms

properties.name: vulnerabilities
properties.description: Temperamental, cognitive, or cultural vulnerabilities

properties.name: maintaining_factors
properties.description: Processes or behaviors maintaining the disorder

properties.name: cultural_factors
properties.description: Cultural or religious influences on formulation

supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has been diagnosed with a specific emotional disorder.

properties.name: diagnosis_date
properties.description: Date of diagnosis

properties.name: diagnostic_criteria
properties.description: Criteria or assessment used

edge_types.description: Links a person (typically a patient) to their individualized treatment plan.

properties.name: start_date
properties.description: Date treatment plan began

properties.name: end_date
properties.description: Date treatment plan ended or is expected to end

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan includes a specific Unified Protocol module.

properties.name: module_sequence
properties.description: Order of the module in the treatment plan

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan is designed to address a specific emotional disorder.
Indicates that a person has been assessed using a specific tool or scale.

properties.name: assessment_date
properties.description: Date of assessment

properties.name: score
properties.description: Score or result of the assessment

edge_types.description: Indicates that a disorder is characterized by a specific symptom.

properties.name: prevalence
properties.description: Prevalence or frequency of the symptom in the disorder

supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders

edge_types.description: Indicates that a UP module is designed to target a specific emotion regulation process.
supported_by_chapters: 1 The Unified Protocol for Transdiagnostic Treatment of Emotional Disorders

edge_types.description: Indicates that a case formulation is created for a specific person (patient).

properties.name: formulation_date
properties.description: Date the formulation was created

edge_types.description: Indicates that a case formulation identifies a specific emotion regulation process as a vulnerability or maintaining factor.
Indicates that a treatment plan has been adapted for a specific cultural or religious context.

properties.name: adaptation_type
properties.description: Type of adaptation (surface or deep structure)

supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person identifies with or is influenced by a specific cultural context.
supported_by_chapters: 16 CrossCultural Applications of the Unified Protocol
